Provisional Irish Republican Army.description: Irish republican paramilitary force that sought to end British rule in Northern Ireland and create an independent socialist republic. It emerged in December 1969 from a split in the IRA and was the most active republican group during the Troubles. It waged a guerrilla and bombing campaign against British forces, killing over 1,700 people. The IRA declared a ceasefire in 1997, decommissioned weapons in 2005, and its political wing Sinn Féin participated in the Good Friday Agreement.

Clan Capriati.description: The Clan Capriati is a criminal organization from Bari, Italy, active since the 1980s. It is primarily involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and gambling management, operating mainly in Bari Vecchia and surrounding areas. Historically led by Antonio Capriati, the clan has seen women take significant roles after arrests of male leaders. In 2023, a major operation dismantled its hierarchical structure, arresting 60 people.

Corleonesi.description: An alliance of Mafia families within the Sicilian Mafia, formed in the 1970s in Corleone, Sicily. Led by Luciano Leggio, Salvatore Riina, Bernardo Provenzano, and others, it took over the Sicilian Mafia Commission and waged the Second Mafia War (1978–1983). The coalition dominated the Mafia until 2006, imposing a quasi-dictatorship over Cosa Nostra.
